text:
This paper examines the spatial configuration of the Polish agricultural sector. Utilizing data from the 2010 Agricultural Census, we categorize six dimensions of internal agricultural drivers: land quality and utilization, agrarian structure, socio-demographic indicators, technical modernization, production patterns, and economic output. Together, these factors provide a framework for indexng the general advancement of the sector and evaluating its structural integrity. 

By mapping these spatial matrices against external variables—such as environmental conditions, historical legacies, urbanization, and Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) funding—this study identifies the primary drivers of regional disparity. The findings reveal that natural conditions play only a minor role in shaping contemporary agricultural patterns. Instead, among human-driven factors, historical determinants remain the most influential influence, maintaining a strong presence even in regions with high absorption of European development funds.
